name: Exposed .env File
description: Detects publicly accessible .env files that may contain database credentials, API keys, and other secrets.
author: AC3 ScanForge
remediation: Remove the .env file from the web root immediately. Add .env to .gitignore. Rotate all exposed credentials. Configure the web server to block access to dotfiles.

intelligence.dfirRelevance: Exposed .env files are a top initial access vector found in DFIR investigations. Attackers use leaked credentials for database access, cloud account takeover, and lateral movement.
